reason: No suitable spawning habitat for salmon, excellent rearing habitat but too steep to access from Coldwater River.
notes: Opportunistic Rainbow Trout spawning habitat present and 70 mm-200 mm Rainbow Trout common in stream. Possible RB/CT hybrid also present. Culvert is curved in middle and undersized.
pscis_assessment_comment: Fish habitat confirmation done in year 2021/08/11. The culvert is bowed 1/3 way from upstream side, after 14m from outlet bends upwards. Culvert curves at bow. Rearing habitat - good, diverse cover composed of overstream vegitation, deep pools, small woody debris, bolders and undercut banks. Spawning habitat - only two suitable gravel pockets observed ~1 sq.m each and 20% of fines (opportunistic use for RB). No overwintering habitat observed. Walked stream ~60m upstream. No suitable habitat for salmon - stream is too steep.

reason: Some rearing habitat values upstream but intermittent stream with low priority from a watershed context.
pscis_assessment_comment: Stream is dewatered. - 1.5m from culvert inlet 0.5m high x 1m long cascade will create access problems when watered. Weir notch is 1m above concrete base. Lip of concrete base is 0.75m above stream bed. Lower base of trapezoid = 2.3m, length = 1.3m, longer trapezoid base = 4.8m. Stream is dewatered downstream of weir to confluence with tributary 58900-2570 downstream (550m). Spawning habitat - none. Overwintering habitat - none. Rearing habitat - fair - stream is stablized by boulders but negatively affected by low flows/dewatering. No suitable spawning habitat for anadromous fish was observed upstream of the confluence with Skuhun C. and the stream supports only a resident rainbow trout.

reason: Good steelhead habitat in reach 1 downstream. Upstream of Manning FSR may be too steep for steelhead rearing. Fires in headwaters may affect habitat quality over medium term.
notes: Steep channel upstream of crossing.
pscis_assessment_comment: Overwintering habitat - none - pools are too small. Rearing habitat - good in step pool morphology provided by boulders, to lesser extent woody debries. Spawning habitat - none - lack of gravels. 2021 forest fire affected stream morphology by reduced crown closure which is slowly being overtaken by shrub growth. Destabilized flow regimes due to lack of live trees and cover on slopes. This is indicated by debries piles, increased bank erosion and sedimentation of channel bed. Confirmed by before and after visual observations from personnel being present on site at both times. Stoneflies still present in stream.

Rearing habitat - good - cover mostly boulders, shallow pools and large woody debris clumps; juvenille fish very abundant upstream and downstream of culvert.

transport_line_structured_name_1: Nicola Kamloops and Similkameen Rail Trail

reason: Active Coho spawning and rearing stream
notes: Culvert and road are a significant sediment source to stream
pscis_assessment_comment: Complete CV assessment done in year 2017/09/14. Culvert complety destroyed, no measurments done. CO presence confirmed above and below crossing. CO sparse upstream of crossing between wetlands. 1st wetland has an excellent overwintering and rearing habitats. Cover composed of DP/ISV, OSV/CB, SWD. Streambed gravelly with combination of good cover stream provides ideal rearing habitat for CO.

Stream is dry, extensive beaver activities throughout of assessed length. Seasonal stream, potentially used as migratory corridor to u/s reaches for ST if spawning habitat exists. The corridor is negatively affected by beaver dams. Existence of spawning habitat should be evaluated - access not granted in 2024. The stream is mismapped by TRIM, it was GPS'd on Oct 10, 2024 from the crossing to the mouth. ~500m section of channel from the mouth is overgrown by perennial plants (grassess and sedges) and is used by cattle as a trail. No alluvium was observed. The stream is flooded only during freshet and may remain watered for ~2 months; however it is likely passable to ST up to the end of Reach 1. Unknown if spawning habitat for ST is present beyond the crossing. The stream may also be used as a refuge or rearing habitat for CO and CH when watered, but is unusable for overwintering. Overall the stream in R1 does not contain preferable habitat for ST or RB.

reason: Important migratory corridor for Coho. Fully passable to adults, passable to juveniles at low flows and 50% at higher fllows.
notes: Major habitat restoration potential at this site.
pscis_assessment_comment: Complete CV assessment done in year 2021/11/03. Calculated water velocity at low flows if the culvert is passable to juvenile fish. Coho and rainbow trout captured above the culvert,passable to fish at low flows. Water velocity calculated at 0.26 m/s.

reason: Important migratory corridor for Coho
notes: Passable to adult fish at all flows. No outlet pool and 0.11 m drop onto rocks likely an impediment to both juveniles and adults. The calculated water velocity is 0.07 m/s. CO fry captured. Water is diverted upstream of Hwy 8 for irrigation purposes during the growing season and regulated by a manual irrigation gate. Most water was diverted during the assessment. Mr. Miller observed CO spawning upstream of Hwy 8 after the gate was opened in the fall.
pscis_assessment_comment: Complete CV assessment done in year 2021/11/03. Water barely flows through CV - water velocity not calculated. Culvert is impassable due to diversion of flow for irrigation purposes. Irrigation gate is located ~150 m upstream. Water drops on rocks at the outlet. In fall the irrigation gate is closed and flow is good. for coho to pass to upper reaches and spawn. CO fry captured upstream.

notes: Approximately 30m upstream form culvert channel splits into two branches. North branch flows through wetland carrying 20% of flow, South Branch follows road ditch carrying 80% of flow. Confluence of branches at UTM - 10.651476. 5560172. Spawning habitat - no spawning due to lack of suitable substrate. Overwintering habitat - downstream of culvert are steady enough flows for overwintering but upstream are reduced flows, and possibly are suitable only at higher flows; unkown flow regime above culvert. Rearing habitat -  stream provides good cover, but upstream N channel flows through wetlands (not preferable habitat for RB). South branch flows along the ditch for at least 50 meters - but unknown source - needs verification. Unknown fish habitat upstream of wetland - stream requires assessment  to determine suitability for RB. Access to property not granted.

reason: Scores as potential barrier. Limited spawning substrates but aquifer provides good flows for rearing and overwintering. More study required to understand salmon use and value

Overwintering habitat - ground fed stream form aquifer offers year round temperature stability with perennial flows - moderate to good overwintering. Rearing habitat - abundant cover composed of instream vegetation, overstream vegetation, and undercut banks.  Perennial flows, but stream banks heavily trampled by cows, pugging visible in stream and results in increased sedimentation and erosion.

structure_list_status: Assessed structure that remains data deficient
reason: Poor quality habitat, flows go subsurface through old quarry rockslide ~500 m upstream from highway. Further impacts from wildfire. Consider revisiting following habitat restoration or recovery.
pscis_assessment_comment: At UTM 10.643347.5513250 stream splits, 1/3 of flow flows North. D=20cm D95=25cm; culvert length taken from GPS'd Outlet and Inlet. Culvert slope taken with clinometer due to Highway 5 traffic. Overwintering habitat does not exist - no suitable pools, seasonal stream; dewatered downstream; severe dewatering occurs in entirety of reach 1 which may be usable for just a few weeks, indicated by cracked soil in channel bed. Rearing habitat value is low; possibly usable in reach 1 for just a few weeks. Reach 1 does not contain suitable instream cover, indicated by D=20cm and complate lack of deep pools and functional large woody debries. In Reach 2 Rearing habitat was good only in 50m section at start of reach (pools, small woody debris, and overhanging vegetation). Spawning habitat - none; seasonal flows in Reach 1, mixed gravel/cobble, compacted channel bed. In reach 2, gradient is too steep. Rises from 4% to 10% within first 100m.
